docs.title: Home
docs.text:
This repository contains the source code, notebooks, and supplementary materials created as part of a master's thesis focused on the analysis of DORIS (Doppler Orbitography and Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite) processing results.

The project provides a modular Python library for:

  • loading DORIS-related data from multiple sources,
  • station coordinate time-series analysis,
  • trend estimation and discontinuity detection,
  • spectral analysis using FFT and Lomb-Scargle periodograms,
  • comparison of precise satellite orbit solutions using Hermite interpolation,
  • RTN transformation and orbit statistics,
  • visualization and export of results.

The library was developed for the analysis of products generated by the DORIS Analysis Center at the Geodetic Observatory Pecný (GOP).

docs.title: Input data
docs.text:
The library supports loading and processing several types of DORIS-related products and auxiliary data.

Supported input sources include:

  • DORIS station coordinate time series,
  • precise satellite orbit products in SP3 format,
  • Bernese GNSS Software outputs,
  • local files,
  • remote files via SSH/SFTP,
  • remote products from NASA CDDIS.

docs.title: Local files
docs.text:
Products already available on a local disk can be copied into the project structure using the local workflow.

from pathlib import Path

from doris.input.local import copy_from_local

result = copy_from_local(
    source_dir=Path("external_products"),
    local_dir=Path("data/orbits"),
    filename_pattern="*.sp3*",
    solution="gop",
    satellite="srl",
    decompress=True,
    keep_compressed=False,
)

print(f"Files processed: {result.count}")
print(f"Files decompressed: {result.count_decompressed}")

The workflow supports:

  • optional filename filtering,
  • automatic decompression,
  • overwrite control,
  • filtering by solution and satellite identifier.

docs.title: SSH / SFTP
docs.text:
Remote products can be downloaded from SSH/SFTP servers.

from pathlib import Path

from doris.input.ssh import download_from_ssh

result = download_from_ssh(
    host="example.server.cz",
    username="user",
    remote_dir="/products/orbits",
    local_dir=Path("data/orbits"),
    filename_pattern="*.sp3.Z",
    solution="gop",
    satellite="srl",
    decompress=True,
)

print(f"Downloaded files: {result.count_downloaded}")

The SSH workflow supports:

  • password authentication,
  • private key authentication,
  • interactive password prompt,
  • stored login credentials,
  • optional filename filtering,
  • automatic decompression,
  • overwrite control,
  • filtering by solution and satellite identifier.

docs.title: NASA CDDIS
docs.text:
DORIS products can be downloaded directly from the NASA CDDIS archive.

from pathlib import Path

from doris.input.cddis import download_from_cddis

result = download_from_cddis(
    technique="doris",
    product="orbits",
    solution="ssa",
    satellite="srl",
    output_root=Path("data"),
    parallel_download=True,
    max_workers=4,
    decompress=True,
)

print(f"Downloaded files: {len(result.downloaded_paths)}")
print(f"Decompressed files: {len(result.decompressed_paths)}")

The CDDIS workflow supports:

  • Earthdata authentication,
  • parallel download,
  • retry handling,
  • MD5SUMS index parsing,
  • automatic archive decompression,
  • overwrite control,
  • filtering by product, solution and satellite identifier.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/kovarmi9/MasterThesis-DorisAnalysis.git
cd MasterThesis-DorisAnalysis
pip install -e .
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Orbit comparison
docs.text: This module provides tools for loading, interpolating and comparing precise satellite orbits.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• loading SP3 orbit products,
  • Hermite interpolation,
  • interpolation to common epochs,
  • comparison of orbit solutions,
  • RTN decomposition,
  • RMS and RMS0 statistics.

docs.title: Loading SP3 orbit products
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.orbits.loading import load_orbit_dataframe

df = load_orbit_dataframe("orbit.sp3")

docs.title: Hermite interpolation
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.orbits.hermite import hermite_at_time

r_interp = hermite_at_time(
    data,
    t_query=12345.0,
    degree=11,
)

docs.title: Interpolation to common epochs
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.orbits.interpolate import interpolate_like

df_interp = interpolate_like(
    df_source=df_ssa,
    df_reference=df_gop,
    degree=11,
    time_col="t_sec",
)

docs.title: Orbit comparison
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.orbits.compare import compare_trajectories

diff_xyz = compare_trajectories(
    df_a=df_ssa,
    df_b=df_gop,
    degree=11,
    rtn=False,
    unit="mm",
)

docs.title: RTN decomposition
docs.text:
diff_rtn = compare_trajectories(
    df_a=df_ssa,
    df_b=df_gop,
    degree=11,
    rtn=True,
    unit="mm",
)

diff_rtn[["dR_mm", "dT_mm", "dN_mm"]]

docs.title: Orbit statistics
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.orbits.stats import orbit_diff_summary

summary = orbit_diff_summary(results)

print(summary)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: This module provides helpers for exporting tables and adjusting plot scales.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• export of pandas DataFrames to LaTeX tables,
  • custom column names and decimal formatting,
  • automatic output paths under LaTeX/tables,
  • preview of generated LaTeX code,
  • shared axis scaling for Matplotlib plots.

docs.title: LaTeX table export
docs.text:
from doris.output.tables import Col, save_latex_table

tex = save_latex_table(
    df,
    "results/stations/licb/trend",
    cols=[
        Col("axis", "Složka", None),
        Col("slope", "Směrnice [mm/rok]", 3),
        Col("r2", "$R^2$", 3),
        Col("bic", "BIC [-]", 1),
    ],
    caption="Lineární trend stanice LICB",
    label="tab:licb_trend",
)

docs.title: Preview LaTeX table
docs.text:
from doris.output.tables import Col, print_latex_table

print_latex_table(
    df,
    cols=[
        Col("axis", "Složka", None),
        Col("slope", "Směrnice [mm/rok]", 3),
        Col("r2", "$R^2$", 3),
    ],
)

docs.title: Build table path
docs.text:
from doris.output.tables import latex_table_path

path = latex_table_path(
    "results",
    "stations",
    "licb",
    filename="trend",
)

docs.title: Plot scale helpers
docs.text:
from doris.output.plots import set_unit_ticks, uniform_y_scale_policy

set_unit_ticks(
    axes,
    step=1.0,
)

uniform_y_scale_policy(
    axes,
    df,
    components=["dE", "dN", "dU"],
)

docs.title: Station analysis
docs.text: This module provides tools for loading and analysing DORIS station coordinate time series.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• loading STCD station files,
  • trend estimation,
  • discontinuity detection,
  • spectral analysis,
  • FFT and Lomb–Scargle periodograms.

docs.title: Loading station data
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.stations.loading import load_station_dataframe

df = load_station_dataframe(
    "station.stcd",
    start="2015-01-01",
    end="2025-12-31",
)

docs.title: Linear trend estimation
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.stations.trend import fit_linear_trend

result = fit_linear_trend(
    df["year"],
    df["dU"],
    sigma=df["sU"],
)

print(result.slope)
print(result.r2)

docs.title: Piecewise trend estimation
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.stations.trend import fit_piecewise_trend

result = fit_piecewise_trend(
    df["year"],
    df["dU"],
    sigma=df["sU"],
    max_segments=2,
)

print(result.breakpoints)

docs.title: FFT periodogram
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.spectral.periodogram import compute_periodogram

fft = compute_periodogram(
    df["year"],
    df["dU"],
    method="fft",
)

docs.title: Lomb–Scargle periodogram
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.spectral.periodogram import compute_periodogram

lsp = compute_periodogram(
    df["year"],
    df["dU"],
    method="lomb_scargle",
)

docs.title: Significant peak detection
docs.text:
from doris.analysis.spectral.significance import (
    estimate_periodogram_threshold,
    find_significant_peaks,
)

threshold = estimate_periodogram_threshold(
    df["year"],
    df["dU"],
    method="lomb_scargle",
)

peaks = find_significant_peaks(
    lsp,
    threshold=threshold,
)

print(peaks)
